verdict.text: Campinas scores Caution. As São Paulo state's second-largest city and an industrial hub, Campinas has significant crime concentrated in peripheral bairros while the commercial centre and affluent south-zone neighbourhoods are more manageable. All three advisories cover it under Brazil's general risk framework.

reconciliation.text: Car theft, armed robbery, and carjacking are the primary risks. Some central bairros (Centro, Cambuí) are reasonable for visitors by day; peripheral zones (DIC, districts north and east) have gang activity and should be avoided entirely.

Business visitors to Campinas should stay in Cambuí, Bosque, or Bonfim Paulista — the city's safer districts. Use ride-hailing throughout. Do not walk in Centro at night. This is not a standard tourist destination; most visitors are here for business or Unicamp connections.

SSP-SP official crime statistics for Campinas region — 2026:
HOMICÍDIO DOLOSO (2): 970 (2026); TENTATIVA DE HOMICÍDIO: 1,631 (2026); LESÃO CORPORAL SEGUIDA DE MORTE: 40 (2026); LESÃO CORPORAL DOLOSA: 73,747 (2026); LESÃO CORPORAL CULPOSA - OUTRAS: 1,426 (2026); LATROCÍNIO: 38 (2026); TOTAL DE ESTUPRO (4): 6,500 (2026); ESTUPRO: 1,514 (2026); ESTUPRO DE VULNERÁVEL: 4,986 (2026); TOTAL DE ROUBO - OUTROS (1): 60,374 (2026); ROUBO - OUTROS: 59,314 (2026); ROUBO DE VEÍCULO: 7,310 (2026); ROUBO DE CARGA: 1,060 (2026); FURTO - OUTROS: 221,268 (2026); FURTO DE VEÍCULO: 33,257 (2026).
reconciliation.source_name: SSP-SP — Secretaria da Segurança Pública do Estado de São Paulo (Campinas)

reconciliation.text: World Bank / UNODC — national intentional homicide rate for Brazil: 2023: 19.3/100k; 2022: 20.1/100k; 2021: 20.5/100k; 2020: 21.8/100k. Note: this is a country-level rate, not city-specific. Source: World Bank Open Data (VC.IHR.PSRC.P5), compiled from UNODC Crime Trends Survey.
